generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T LUFFA. Dried fruit of a Brazilian plant called Cabacinha. Authority. (Dr. Scott Alison, Pharm. Journ., vol. iv, 1845, Berridge's Collection. New Engl. Med. Gaz., 1876, p. 303). 1 , a man, aged thirty years, took 1 grain extract; 2 , a woman, took 40 drops tincture. ► Within one quarter of an hour he was overcome with faintness and nausea. He swallowed a little warm water, and immediately vomited freely. No violent action of the bowels followed, but for some days afterwards the stools were more than usually copious, [1] . ► She had nausea for two days and retched a little, [2] .
